title: Searching Algorithms
analogy: Linear search is like flipping through every page of a book to find a word. Binary search is like opening to the middle, checking if you're too early or too late, and halving your search range.

overview.summary: Searching algorithms find the location of a target value within a data structure. Linear search scans sequentially; binary search halves the range on sorted data.
learningObjectives: Understand when linear search is necessary vs when binary search applies.
Implement binary search correctly (avoiding off-by-one errors).
Recognize search as a subroutine in larger algorithms.
overview.interviewRelevance: Search is a fundamental primitive. Binary search alone appears in 10%+ of coding interviews.

paragraphs: Databases use B-tree search (a generalized binary search) to find records among millions of rows in milliseconds.
Git bisect uses binary search to find the exact commit that introduced a bug.
realWorldHook.ahaMoment: Binary search reduces a million-element search to just 20 comparisons — that's the power of halving.

definition.technicalDefinition: Searching is the process of finding a specific element in a collection. Linear search examines each element sequentially. Binary search requires sorted data and eliminates half the remaining elements each step.

vocabulary.definition: Check each element one by one. O(N) time.
Repeatedly halve the search range on sorted data. O(log N) time.
The set of possible positions where the target could be.

keyProperties: Linear search works on unsorted data: O(N).
Binary search requires sorted data: O(log N).
Binary search can be applied to abstract search spaces (not just arrays).

operations.name: Linear Search
operations.description: Scan from left to right, comparing each element to the target.
operations.visualNote: Pointer moves left to right, each cell compared and dimmed if not a match.

operations.name: Binary Search
operations.description: Compare target to middle element, then search left or right half accordingly.
operations.visualNote: The search range halves with each step, the middle element flashes before elimination.

practice.title: Search Insert Position
practice.keyInsight: Binary search for the insertion point.

practice.title: Find First and Last Position
practice.keyInsight: Two binary searches: one for left bound, one for right bound.

practice.title: Search a 2D Matrix
practice.keyInsight: Treat the 2D matrix as a flat sorted array.

practice.title: Find Minimum in Rotated Sorted Array
practice.keyInsight: Binary search comparing mid to right end.

conceptQuiz.question: Binary search requires what precondition?
conceptQuiz.explanation: Binary search relies on sorted order to eliminate half the data at each step.

recognitionQuiz.problemStatement: Find a specific element in a sorted array of 10 million numbers as fast as possible.
recognitionQuiz.explanation: Binary search on sorted data gives O(log N) = ~23 comparisons for 10M elements.
